Part B.key_evidence: Positive difference detection in g-band at magpsf=20.37 with reasonable S/N (sigmapsf=0.13). Nearest PS1 source is 0.72 arcsec away with sgscore1~0.007 (galaxy-like) and faint PS1 magnitudes (~20-21), indicating a likely host galaxy. ndethist=6 detections over deltajd~25 days suggests a persistent but new transient on top of the faint host. classtar=0.909 indicates a point-like morphology in the candidate stamp. The difference image shows a localized positive residual at the center.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Most consistent with a supernova: a point-like, positive-flux transient slightly offset from a faint galaxy-like PS1 host (distpsnr1=0.72", sgscore1~0), detected multiple times over ~25 days — typical SN timescale and host association.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An AGN is possible if the nearby PS1 source is the nucleus of a host galaxy, though sgscore1 near zero and faint host magnitudes make this less compelling than SN with only 6 detections and no prior history evident. A nuclear transient in a faint galaxy cannot be fully excluded.
